paragraphs.text: ESSNA
EUROPEAN SPECIALIST SPORTS NUTRITION ALLIANCE

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
Introduction
paragraphs.text: This document outlines the European Specialist Sports Nutrition Alliance (ESSNA)'s response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act. While social media has played a key role in driving the growth of the sports nutrition industry, it has also introduced risks, with some influencers promoting non-compliant products with unauthorised ingredients and unauthorised medicinal or health claims. Such practices can have serious consequences for consumers, whose health and safety may be compromised, and for responsible sports nutrition companies complying with EU rules, which are placed at an unfair competitive disadvantage. ESSNA therefore welcomes the European Commission's initiative to introduce a Digital Fairness Act and supports the importance of tackling harmful online practices. To address these risks, ESSNA calls for stronger enforcement of EU food law on social media platforms, the development of an EU-wide code of conduct for influencer marketing, and enhanced consumer guidance and digital literacy to help citizens critically assess health and nutrition claims.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
The role of ESSNA in compliance
paragraphs.text: Consumer health and safety is a key priority for ESSNA and its members. Launched in 2013, our non-compliance campaign seeks to ensure that products on the market comply with EU legislation. Common issues include products containing banned substances, unauthorised novel foods, or making unsubstantiated health claims. We work with major regulatory and enforcement bodies, including Europol, to assist in the enforcement of legislation and ensure that consumer health is safeguarded. Since the campaign was launched ESSNA has identified and resolved over 600 cases of non-compliance.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
Policy gaps to be addressed by the Digital Fairness Act
Consumer safety risks from unauthorised products and claims
paragraphs.text: Influencers increasingly promote products on social media containing banned substances, unauthorised ingredients, or making unauthorised health and nutrition claims, creating serious risks for consumers. EU law strictly regulates food ingredients and the health claims associated with them to ensure consumer protection. Only ingredients approved under EU law may be marketed, and health claims must be authorised by EFSA and communicated in approved language. Ingredients that have not undergone assessment or approval may have unpredictable or harmful physical and psychological effects. Companies using unauthorised health claims mislead consumers about the safety or benefits of their products, endangering public health, distorting fair competition, and undermining trust in legitimate businesses and the EU regulatory framework.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
Policy gaps to be addressed by the Digital Fairness Act
Consumer safety risks from unauthorised products and claims
paragraphs.text: One of the most concerning examples is the promotion of illegal doping substances, such as the steroid trenbolone, which has become popular with teenagers through influencer-led trends on platforms such as TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube. While these substances were once sold through hidden networks, influencers now play a key role in their diffusion, making them more accessible and socially accepted. The lack of enforcement of EU law on social media platforms poses serious risks to consumer safety and undermines the sector's reputation. Specific examples of this issue can be provided by the ESSNA Secretariat upon request.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
Policy gaps to be addressed by the Digital Fairness Act
Consumer safety risks from unauthorised products and claims
paragraphs.text: Social media platforms provide insufficient oversight to ensure all content complies with EU rules, and they remain a grey area of enforcement under the Digital Services Act. Social media platforms used to sell products should therefore be required to take greater responsibility for ensuring compliance and protecting consumers.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: ESSNA calls for targeted measures to strengthen online safety and protect consumers, ensuring that sports nutrition products can be used confidently and safely. Our key recommendations include:

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: Strengthen enforcement under the Digital Services Act (DSA)

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: Clarify enforcement responsibilities by issuing guidance to ensure that promotion of unauthorised or illegal products by influencers falls clearly within the scope of the DSA's illegal content provisions.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: ESSNA

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: EUROPEAN SPECIALIST SPORTS NUTRITION ALLIANCE

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: Require stricter platform accountability by obliging Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) to proactively monitor and remove posts that promote unauthorised products or make unauthorised health or nutrition claims.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
ESSNA's Recommendations
paragraphs.text: Establish clear reporting mechanisms for consumers and authorities to flag illegal or misleading product promotions, with mandatory follow-up actions by platforms.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Introduce an EU-wide code of conduct for influencer marketing
paragraphs.text: Develop, in cooperation with consumer and industry associations, a voluntary but Commission-endorsed code of conduct setting out minimum standards for influencers and brands promoting food, supplements, and other regulated products.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Introduce an EU-wide code of conduct for influencer marketing
paragraphs.text: Include requirements for transparency, such as clear labelling of paid partnerships and mandatory disclosure of sponsorships linked to product promotion.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
3. Enhance consumer awareness and digital literacy
paragraphs.text: Produce guidance to help citizens critically assess and understand health and nutrition claims.

section_path: ESSNA's position on the Digital Fairness Act
About ESSNA
paragraphs.text: The European Specialist Sports Nutrition Alliance (ESSNA) is the leading European trade association for the sports and active nutrition industry. It brings together major manufacturers and distributors of sports nutrition products, suppliers of ingredients, as well as specialist scientific and legal consultants advising the sector. ESSNA was established in 2003 to promote a regulatory and policy environment that promotes innovation and competitiveness for the sector and protect sports nutrition consumers and their needs. Today, ESSNA is a mature and respected organisation, widely recognised as the voice of the responsible specialist sports and active nutrition sector in Europe.
